title: Orientations of Proteins in Membranes database

infobox.value: The database provides spatial arrangement of [proteins](/wiki/Proteins "Proteins") in the [lipid bilayer](/wiki/Lipid_bilayer "Lipid bilayer")
[Protein structures](/wiki/Protein_structure "Protein structure") from the [PDB](/wiki/Protein_Data_Bank "Protein Data Bank")
All
[University of Michigan College of Pharmacy](/wiki/University_of_Michigan_College_of_Pharmacy "University of Michigan College of Pharmacy")
[PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [16397007](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16397007)
modified [PDB format](/wiki/Protein_Data_Bank_(file_format) "Protein Data Bank (file format)")
<https://opm.phar.umich.edu>
[OPM files](https://opm.phar.umich.edu/download.php)
[Server for calculating positions of proteins in membranes](https://opm.phar.umich.edu/server.php)
CC-BY 3.0
Curated

(**OPM**) **database** provides spatial positions of [membrane protein](/wiki/Membrane_protein "Membrane protein") [structures](/wiki/Protein_structure "Protein structure") with respect to the [lipid bilayer](/wiki/Lipid_bilayer "Lipid bilayer").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-OPM-cite-2)[[3]](#cite_note-OPM1-3)[[4]](#cite_note-peripheral-4) Positions of the proteins are calculated using an [implicit solvation model](/wiki/Implicit_solvation "Implicit solvation") of the lipid bilayer.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) The results of calculations were verified against experimental studies of spatial arrangement of [transmembrane](/wiki/Transmembrane_protein "Transmembrane protein") and [peripheral](/wiki/Peripheral_membrane_protein "Peripheral membrane protein") proteins in membranes.[[4]](#cite_note-peripheral-4)[[7]](#cite_note-Malmberg-7)[[8]](#cite_note-Spencer-8)[[9]](#cite_note-Lathrop-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Kuta-10)[[11]](#cite_note-Tatulian-11)[[12]](#cite_note-Hristova-12)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Database of protein structures

Orientations of Proteins in Membranes

| Content | |
| --- | --- |
| Description | The database provides spatial arrangement of [proteins](/wiki/Proteins "Proteins") in the [lipid bilayer](/wiki/Lipid_bilayer "Lipid bilayer") |
| Data types captured | [Protein structures](/wiki/Protein_structure "Protein structure") from the [PDB](/wiki/Protein_Data_Bank "Protein Data Bank") |
| [Organisms](/wiki/Organism "Organism") | All |
| Contact | |
| [Research center](/wiki/Research_center "Research center") | [University of Michigan College of Pharmacy](/wiki/University_of_Michigan_College_of_Pharmacy "University of Michigan College of Pharmacy") |
| Primary citation | [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [16397007](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16397007) |
| Release date | 2005 |
| Access | |
| [Data format](/wiki/File_format "File format") | modified [PDB format](/wiki/Protein_Data_Bank_(file_format) "Protein Data Bank (file format)") |
| Website | <https://opm.phar.umich.edu> |
| Download URL | [OPM files](https://opm.phar.umich.edu/download.php) |
| Tools | |
| [Web](/wiki/Web_application "Web application") | [Server for calculating positions of proteins in membranes](https://opm.phar.umich.edu/server.php) |
| Miscellaneous | |

| Curation policy | Curated |

**Orientations of Proteins in Membranes** (**OPM**) **database** provides spatial positions of [membrane protein](/wiki/Membrane_protein "Membrane protein") [structures](/wiki/Protein_structure "Protein structure") with respect to the [lipid bilayer](/wiki/Lipid_bilayer "Lipid bilayer").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-OPM-cite-2)[[3]](#cite_note-OPM1-3)[[4]](#cite_note-peripheral-4) Positions of the proteins are calculated using an [implicit solvation model](/wiki/Implicit_solvation "Implicit solvation") of the lipid bilayer.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) The results of calculations were verified against experimental studies of spatial arrangement of [transmembrane](/wiki/Transmembrane_protein "Transmembrane protein") and [peripheral](/wiki/Peripheral_membrane_protein "Peripheral membrane protein") proteins in membranes.[[4]](#cite_note-peripheral-4)[[7]](#cite_note-Malmberg-7)[[8]](#cite_note-Spencer-8)[[9]](#cite_note-Lathrop-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Kuta-10)[[11]](#cite_note-Tatulian-11)[[12]](#cite_note-Hristova-12)

Proteins structures are taken from the [Protein Data Bank](/wiki/Protein_Data_Bank "Protein Data Bank"). OPM also provides structural classification of membrane-associated proteins into families and superfamilies, [membrane topology](/wiki/Membrane_topology "Membrane topology"), [quaternary structure](/wiki/Quaternary_structure "Quaternary structure") of proteins in membrane-bound state, and the type of a destination [membrane](/wiki/Biological_membrane "Biological membrane") for each protein. The coordinate files with calculated membrane boundaries are downloadable. The site allows visualization of protein structures with membrane boundary planes through [Jmol](/wiki/Jmol "Jmol").

The database was widely used in experimental and theoretical studies of membrane-associated proteins.[[13]](#cite_note-park-helms-13)[[14]](#cite_note-marsh-jost-peggion-14)[[15]](#cite_note-pfb-2007-15)[[16]](#cite_note-cyl-2006-16)[[17]](#cite_note-pbm-2006-17) However, structures of many membrane-associated proteins are not included in the database if their spatial arrangement in membrane can not be computationally predicted from the three-dimensional structure. This is the case when all membrane-anchoring parts of the proteins ([amphiphilic](/wiki/Amphiphilic "Amphiphilic") [alpha helices](/wiki/Alpha_helices "Alpha helices"), exposed nonpolar residues, or [lipidated amino acid residues](/wiki/Lipid_anchored_protein "Lipid anchored protein")) are missing in the experimental structures.[[4]](#cite_note-peripheral-4) The database also does not include lower resolution structures with only main chain atoms provided by the [Protein Data Bank](/wiki/Protein_Data_Bank "Protein Data Bank"). The calculated spatial arrangements of the lower resolution protein structures in the lipid bilayer can be found in other resources, such as PDBTM.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
